title: Aureline semantic-workspace-graph public query-family and result-envelope boundary schema
description: Boundary schema for the public query-family and result-envelope surface of the shared semantic-workspace-graph. This schema publishes one stable query-family vocabulary and one versioned result-envelope shape every consumer reuses — search, docs, AI context assembly, review packs, support bundles, and the eventual public graph query API — so no surface leaks an ad hoc internal query shape into a future public contract. A query family is a named safe inspect / read / query group with frozen rules: which scope classes it may read, which identity durability classes it may emit, which edge durability classes it may emit, which readiness states it tolerates, which result-truth classes it renders, whether it may assemble AI context, whether it is publishable on the public graph query API, and whether its result envelope is support-export safe. The canonical node / edge / provenance / freshness / confidence / scope / anchor / citation vocabularies are owned by schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json; the published identity and edge boundary contracts are owned by schemas/graph/node_identity.schema.json and schemas/graph/edge_family.schema.json; the result-truth / readiness / ranking-reason vocabulary is owned by schemas/search/search_result_truth.schema.json. This schema references those canonical types and adds the query-family descriptor, the result-envelope record, the query-invocation record, and the query-family audit-event record. Raw query bodies, raw source bytes, raw symbol definitions, raw document bodies, and raw provider URLs never cross this boundary. Adding a new query_family_id, a new query_safety_class, a new query_intent_class, a new result_row_class, a new published query-family constraint id, or a new query-family audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps query_family_schema_version. Repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

query_family_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this query-family export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

result_envelope_version.description: Integer version on the result_envelope_record payload shape. A consumer MUST refuse a result envelope whose result_envelope_version is greater than the consumer's supported max. Bumped on breaking envelope-shape changes.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

query_family_id.description: Frozen public query-family id vocabulary. Mirrors workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#/$defs/query_family_tag so a node or edge's query_family_tags list directly names the query families it is intended to serve.

query_safety_class.description: Published query-safety class naming the 'safe inspect / read / query group' a family belongs to. inspect_read_only: the query never mutates state and never materializes derived rows. read_durable_only: the query may render rows only when identity_durability_class = durable_identity and edge_durability_class = durable_edge. read_derived_permitted: the query may render derived, inferred, or annotated rows but MUST mark them with the derived chip. walk_scope_bounded: the query walks edges but MUST stop at workset_scope boundaries and MUST render policy_view_node projections instead of revealing hidden members. ai_context_restricted: the query assembles AI context and MUST obey the redaction / no-raw-body rule and MUST cite via explainer_citation_slot. support_export_bundle: the query produces a support-export-safe envelope (no raw credentials, no raw provider URLs, no raw source bytes). public_api_safe: the query is publishable on the public graph API; result shape MUST remain versioned.

query_intent_class.description: Published query-intent class. Pairs with query_family_id to describe what the query is actually trying to do. lookup_by_identity: resolve a specific node_id or edge_id. lookup_by_label: find rows whose display_label or qualified_path matches a token (labels only, never raw bodies). walk_neighbors: return directly connected nodes. walk_typed_edges: return edges of a given edge_class or edge_family_group. collect_impact_set: return the impacts set for a subject node. collect_context_set: gather an AI context set with explainer citations. enumerate_scope_members: list nodes inside a workset_scope_node or policy_view_node. enumerate_citations: list explainer_citation_slot targets for a subject node. export_support_bundle_slice: emit the support-export envelope for a subject set.

result_row_class.description: Published result-row class. node_identity_row: the row projects a node_identity_record. edge_family_row: the row projects an edge_family_record. missing_anchor_row: the row projects an honest-absence row pointing at a missing_anchor_node. policy_hidden_row: the row projects a policy_view_node with hidden_member_count only. scope_partial_row: the row projects a node or edge whose scope visibility is partial_visible. warming_row: the row projects a warming freshness frame with a warming_progress_hint. stale_row: the row projects a non-authoritative freshness frame with a typed stale_reason.

readiness_state.description: Published readiness-state vocabulary. Re-exported from schemas/search/search_result_truth.schema.json so the result envelope shares one readiness vocabulary with every search surface; hot_set_ready, partial_index, warm_index, stale_index, reindexing, and index_unavailable envelopes MUST carry a partial-truth disclosure.

result_truth_class.description: Published result-truth vocabulary. Re-exported from schemas/search/search_result_truth.schema.json. heuristic and hybrid envelopes MUST carry a partial-truth disclosure and MUST NOT render alone as authoritative.

disclosure_class.description: Frozen partial-truth disclosure classes the consumer renders as copy. Consumers MUST NOT hide the disclosure when the envelope carries one.

reader_copy.description: Redaction-aware reader copy the consumer renders verbatim. Never embeds raw query bodies, raw source bytes, or raw provider URLs.

partial_truth_disclosure.description: One partial-truth disclosure. Required on every result envelope whose readiness_state is not fully_indexed or whose result_truth_class is heuristic or hybrid.

safety_classes.description: One or more safety classes this family belongs to. A family's safety classes constrain the envelopes it may emit.

allowed_node_classes.description: Published allow-list of node classes this family may render. A row outside the allow-list MUST NOT appear in an envelope from this family.

emits_ai_context.description: True when the family is allowed to assemble AI context; such families MUST belong to safety class ai_context_restricted and MUST emit explainer_citation_slot refs rather than raw bodies.

public_api_publishable.description: True when the family is publishable on the public graph query API; such families MUST belong to safety class public_api_safe and MUST lock the result_envelope_version.

support_export_safe.description: True when the family may produce support-export envelopes; such families MUST belong to safety class support_export_bundle and MUST preserve redaction.

description_note.description: Redaction-aware reviewer note describing what the family is for. Never embeds raw source bytes, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

query_family_descriptor_record.description: One query-family descriptor. Consumers read this record to decide whether a given query family may emit a given row, render a given truth class, or publish on a given boundary.

subject_node_ids.description: Opaque graph-node ids the query is about. Never a raw query body; never a raw source or URL.

label_token.description: Optional redaction-aware label token when the intent is lookup_by_label. Consumers MUST NOT render raw user input here; label tokens are the producer's normalized form.

scope_refs.description: The workset / scope the query is bounded by. A walk_scope_bounded family stops at these boundaries.

query_invocation_record.description: One query invocation. Envelopes cite the invocation id so readers can reconstruct 'which query produced this envelope' without inventing a private request shape.

row_index.description: Stable zero-based index inside the envelope. Consumers cite (envelope_id, row_index) to anchor a row without inventing a private row handle.

node_identity_ref.description: Opaque node_id when row_class = node_identity_row, missing_anchor_row, policy_hidden_row, scope_partial_row, warming_row, or stale_row. The consumer resolves the full node_identity_record against schemas/graph/node_identity.schema.json.

edge_family_ref.description: Opaque edge_id when row_class = edge_family_row. The consumer resolves the full edge_family_record against schemas/graph/edge_family.schema.json.

row_note.description: Redaction-aware reviewer note for this row. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

result_row.description: One published result row. Rows reference full node_identity and edge_family records by id rather than re-carrying the full record; the consumer joins against the published identity / family records for full fields.

rows.description: Ordered result rows. Row indexing is stable inside the envelope; consumers cite (envelope_id, row_index) as the public anchor.

partial_truth_disclosure.description: Required when readiness_state is not fully_indexed or result_truth_class is heuristic or hybrid.

envelope_note.description: Redaction-aware envelope-level note. Never embeds raw query bodies, raw source bytes, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

result_envelope_record.description: One published result envelope. Every surface that renders, logs, exports, or replays a graph-query result reads this shape. The envelope is versioned (query_family_schema_version + result_envelope_version) so consumers refuse a newer envelope rather than guessing, and the row anchor is (envelope_id, row_index) so no ad hoc internal query row shape leaks into the public contract.

published_query_family_constraint_id.description: Published query-family / envelope constraint ids consumers cite verbatim when reporting a contract violation.

query_family_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event id vocabulary for the query-family / result-envelope surface. Support bundles, replay captures, and AI evidence packets cite these verbatim.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref into structured notes. MUST NOT embed raw source bodies, raw query bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.
